codeReview.summary: Pass. I reviewed the feature metadata, handoff, transcript skeleton, the main feature commit `0a68b84`, the follow-up fix commit `8dfbfe0`, and the current dashboard/API files that drive workspace resolution. The implementation satisfies the feature contract and tenancy-UX expectations: `apps/dashboard/src/components/team-dropdown.tsx` renders a non-interactive current-workspace label for single-team users, a Midday-shaped dropdown with a checked current workspace and alternate switch targets for multi-team users, and on successful `user.switchTeam` mutation it invalidates cached queries and calls `router.refresh()` so the header shell and protected `privateData` reads stay aligned with the newly active membership. The supporting API primitives in `packages/api/src/lib/team.ts` and `packages/api/src/routers/{team,user}.ts` preserve the membership-first switch contract from the architecture guide, and the `DropdownMenuGroup` follow-up fix removes the Base UI invariant that previously crashed the menu. I did not find a blocking or non-blocking code defect within this feature scope.

sharedStateObservations.observation: This handoff's `commitId` only captures the follow-up fix commit, not the full feature span, so a review process that inspects only `handoff.commitId` would miss most of the implementation work.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: /home/louis/.factory/missions/bf937643-326f-44d3-a49d-533fd744ae00/handoffs/2026-04-05T21-52-56-282Z__team-switcher-and-shell-alignment__4e7de3ac-227e-4178-8fc0-16ee0c27c3b3.json:6 records `commitId: 8dfbfe0`, while :11 says the feature created `apps/dashboard/src/lib/team-switcher.ts`, `team-switcher.test.ts`, `components/team-dropdown.tsx`, and updated `header.tsx`. `git show --stat 8dfbfe0` changes only `apps/dashboard/src/components/team-dropdown.tsx`; `git show --stat 0a68b84` contains the actual feature addition across those four files.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The `dashboard-parity-worker` skill still reports `followedProcedure: true` even when one of its required skill invocations (`ultracite`) was skipped in the transcript.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: /home/louis/developer/faworra/faworra-new/.factory/skills/dashboard-parity-worker/SKILL.md:20-24 lists `midday-reference`, `agent-browser`, and `ultracite` as required skills. Parsing /home/louis/.factory/missions/bf937643-326f-44d3-a49d-533fd744ae00/worker-transcripts.jsonl for session `4e7de3ac-227e-4178-8fc0-16ee0c27c3b3` shows only `mission-worker-base`, `dashboard-parity-worker`, `midday-reference`, and `agent-browser` skill invocations, while the handoff at /home/louis/.factory/missions/bf937643-326f-44d3-a49d-533fd744ae00/handoffs/2026-04-05T21-52-56-282Z__team-switcher-and-shell-alignment__4e7de3ac-227e-4178-8fc0-16ee0c27c3b3.json:101-104 still records `followedProcedure: true`.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The dashboard worker skill says to run the scoped validators from `.factory/services.yaml`, but the manifest's validator scope is broader than what this feature actually ran, so workers keep falling back to ad hoc feature-specific commands instead of the declared shared contract.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: /home/louis/developer/faworra/faworra-new/.factory/services.yaml:4-6 defines `typecheck`, `lint`, and `test` over `apps/dashboard/src`, `apps/api/src`, `packages/api/src`, `packages/auth/src`, `packages/db/src`, and `packages/env/src`. The same skill file says at :43 to run validators from `.factory/services.yaml`, but the handoff for this feature only records `bun test --max-concurrency=6 apps/dashboard/src packages/api/src`, `bun x ultracite check apps/dashboard/src packages/api/src`, and `bun x tsc -p apps/dashboard/tsconfig.json --noEmit` at lines 14-27.

summary: Pass. I reviewed the transcript skeleton, handoff, the main implementation commit plus the follow-up fix, and the relevant dashboard/API files. The workspace switcher behavior is correct for single-team and multi-team users, the switch path stays membership-first and keeps shell/protected queries aligned after refresh/navigation, and I only noted shared-state gaps around handoff commit-span reporting and skill/service procedure drift.
